# Project Governance Overview

## Roles

| Role        | Description                                                                                               | Access Level                          |
| ----------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| User        | Individuals who use the software                                                                          | None                                  |
| Supporter   | Contributes by commenting on Issues/PRs, or opening Pull Requests                                         | Submitting PRs from forks             |
| Contributor | Has 2 merged pull requests or have meaningful commenting contributions                                    | Write access to non-critical branches |
| Core Member | Selected by core members / maintainers based on significant contributions and decision-making involvement | Write access to important branches    |
| Maintainer  | Reserved for project creators and long-time core members with at least 1 year of consistent contribution  | Admin access to the project           |

<sub>If you believe you qualify for one of these roles but have not been granted the corresponding access level, please reach out to us <a href="mailto:support@enygmah.com" rel="noopener noreferrer">via email</a> or on our <a href="https://github.com/hotaydev/enygmah/discussions" rel="noopener noreferrer">Github Discussions Page</a>.</sub>

## Decision-Making

- **Platform:** [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/hotaydev/enygmah/discussions)
- **Decision Method:** Upvoting and Downvoting
- **Voting Mechanism:** All votes carry equal weight
- **Hierarchy:** Used for security purposes only; all votes have the same value regardless of role.

## Principles

### 1. Open

- All of our software is open source.

### 2. Welcoming and Respectful

- Look at the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/hotaydev/enygmah/blob/main/.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

### 3. Transparent and Accessible

- Changes are made publicly in the code repository.

### 4. Equality

- All decisions are made through voting, ensuring equal representation.
- Ideas are proposed through [Discussions](https://github.com/hotaydev/enygmah/discussions) for community voting.

## New Contributors

- New contributors are welcomed, assisted with the PR workflow, and directed to relevant documentation and communication channels.

## Retiring

- Life priorities, interests, and passions can change, so maintainers can retire sometimes.
- Retiring maintainers should inform others, help find replacements, or help with the continuity of their work.
- Inactive maintainers for 12 months can be removed after previous e-mail notification and, if needed, approval from at least two active maintainers.
- Emeritus maintainers can regain active status by renewing contributions, subject to approval by at least two active maintainers.

## Changes in Governance

- Changes can be initiated by opening a GitHub pull request.

## Continuous Improvement

- Open to improvements and suggestions from the community to enhance the governance model.

**Note:** The hierarchical access levels are implemented for security purposes only, and the equality principle is maintained through equal voting rights for all community members, regardless of their roles.
